>

documents.content:
Subject: Re: SERVICE Jane Kay Cortez vs Solomon Samuel Barber, Benjamin Jay Barber vs Solomon Samuel Barber
Folder: [Gmail]/All Mail
From: Alex Bluestone <alex@salemprobate.com>
To: benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
Date: Sat, 04 Apr 2026 16:38:28 +0000
Matched addresses: all mailbox messages
Artifact directory: /home/barberb/HACC/evidence/email_imports/solomon-live-fetch-2026/starworks5/gmail-import/0000000-0000999/msg_CO1PR10MB44826703707F0BD1A5D81F64AC5FA-CO1PR10MB4482.namprd10.prod.outlook.com_20260404_Re-SERVICE-Jane-Kay-Cortez-vs-Solomon-Samuel-Barber-Benjamin-Jay-Barber-vs-Solom

Mr. Barber,

Please be aware that I do not accept service on behalf of my client. I represent my client only as it relates to the guardianship of Jane Cortez.

If you are represented by counsel, please be advised that I must speak directly with counsel and not you. If you are not represented please advise of the same.

Get Outlook for Android<https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg>
________________________________
From: benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
Sent: Friday, April 3, 2026 3:17:26 PM
To: Alex Bluestone <alex@salemprobate.com>
Subject: SERVICE Jane Kay Cortez vs Solomon Samuel Barber, Benjamin Jay Barber vs Solomon Samuel Barber


[https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png] sam barber restraining order.pdf<https://drive.google.com/file/d/1hhzVVCLj1LXYKhm9-enQ5R-CzFzNoXwJ/view?usp=drive_web>

Attached is a restraining order that your client has been avoiding being served on, I have sent a paper copy of the same restraining order to your office via FedEx. Consistent with your duty of honesty to the court, you should have mentioned that there was already an ongoing case or controversy between the same parties, and that it was subject to collateral estoppel.

However I believe that there is reason to believe that your client was less than honest with you, given that he has previously expressed his opinion that he was free to violate a restraining order, because it had no effect while he was avoiding service, and he might have hid that fact.

Many of the problems that he complains about in his petition to the court, are harms that derived from him attempting to usurp power as a power of attorney, for example temporarily having me removed from the lease of our apartment, despite the fact he was under a protective order.

You should now make efforts to ensure that he preserves all evidence that is related to the parties to the controversy, and third parties that are related in some way or another with the parties to the controversy, regardless of whether those records are in your possession, possession of your agents, or other third parties. This would include GPS and positioning data, phone calls, emails, letters, and any other record such as contemporaneous notes, meeting minutes, or contracts, that may exist.

It is my intention to send the court a motion to show cause why you / your have failed to appear in the restraining order case, and a motion to show cause why your client has refused to turn over documents and follow the directives of the court, and I suggest that you have a "Back behind the woodshed" moment with your client regarding honesty and candor with the courts.

[cid:ii_19d556249e1ac8c9ded1]

Subject: SERVICE Jane Kay Cortez vs Solomon Samuel Barber, Benjamin Jay Barber vs Solomon Samuel Barber
Folder: [Gmail]/All Mail
From: benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
To: alex@salemprobate.com
Date: Fri, 03 Apr 2026 15:17:26 -0700
Matched addresses: all mailbox messages
Artifact directory: /home/barberb/HACC/evidence/email_imports/solomon-live-fetch-2026/starworks5/gmail-import/0000000-0000999/msg_CAMTdTS--K-3f4FAEGwKV5TY9qYTnsswDvLK_1XjMwQ50VY4ENw-mail.gmail.com_20260403_SERVICE-Jane-Kay-Cortez-vs-Solomon-Samuel-Barber-Benjamin-Jay-Barber-vs-Solomon-

sam barber restraining order.pdf
<https://drive.google.com/file/d/1hhzVVCLj1LXYKhm9-enQ5R-CzFzNoXwJ/view?usp=drive_web>

Attached is a restraining order that your client has been avoiding being
served on, I have sent a paper copy of the same restraining order to your
office via FedEx. Consistent with your duty of honesty to the court, you
should have mentioned that there was already an ongoing case or controversy
between the same parties, and that it was subject to collateral estoppel.

However I believe that there is reason to believe that your client was less
than honest with you, given that he has previously expressed his opinion
that he was free to violate a restraining order, because it had no effect
while he was avoiding service, and he might have hid that fact.

Many of the problems that he complains about in his petition to the court,
are harms that derived from him attempting to usurp power as a power of
attorney, for example temporarily having me removed from the lease of our
apartment, despite the fact he was under a protective order.

You should now make efforts to ensure that he preserves all evidence that
is related to the parties to the controversy, and third parties that are
related in some way or another with the parties to the controversy,
regardless of whether those records are in your possession, possession of
your agents, or other third parties. This would include GPS and positioning
data, phone calls, emails, letters, and any other record such as
contemporaneous notes, meeting minutes, or contracts, that may exist.

It is my intention to send the court a motion to show cause why you / your
have failed to appear in the restraining order case, and a motion to show
cause why your client has refused to turn over documents and follow the
directives of the court, and I suggest that you have a "Back behind the
woodshed" moment with your client regarding honesty and candor with the
courts.

Subject: Silicon Florist 🌹 More QSBS BS 💩
Folder: [Gmail]/All Mail
From: Rick Turoczy via LinkedIn <newsletters-noreply@linkedin.com>
To: Benjamin Barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Apr 2026 23:19:51 +0000
Matched addresses: all mailbox messages
Artifact directory: /home/barberb/HACC/evidence/email_imports/solomon-live-fetch-2026/starworks5/gmail-import/0000000-0000999/msg_266019719.5240824.1775171991981-ltx1-app3322.prod.linkedin.com_20260402_Silicon-Florist-More-QSBS-BS

Startup news: Portland Oregon

By Rick Turoczy


By Rick Turoczy

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/silicon-florist-more-qsbs-bs-rick-turoczy-ycw0e?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BYYpwcpvDR9y1zep%2FQMvMCw%3D%3D&midToken=AQGEpmBPZVOktg&midSig=2HGYGqpBsnsYc1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-4xcaz4~mni39zk7~mt-null-null&eid=4xcaz4-mni39zk7-mt
  
Issue 845 | Have me read it to you | Get the newsletter on time My response to the Oregon Governor’s Prosperity Council Thanks for putting up with my constant cajoling on the Oregon Governor’s Prosperity Council survey. I hope you got the chance to respond. Admittedly, I didn’t want to jaundice your response with my response. But now that the submissions have closed I wanted to share what I submitted. Again, my response was very myopically focused on the Portland startup community, as is my wont. ... More Oregon QSBS tax reform could work — or it could create a $1B hole I complained about the whole QSBS stuff in my own way. And I provided a very subjective perspective to the Oregon Prosperity Council. But someone finally did the math on SB 1507, the Oregon bill that disentangles Qualified Small Business Stock tax shelters from the federal tax law and establishes a 9.9% tax on QSBS returns. And — shocker — the numbers are worse than I thought. ... More All Raise needs your perspective on the current state of VC I know I’ve been asking a lot of you lately. Surveys. Feedback forms. Attending things and stuff. Admittedly, I feel guilty. You’re already overly busy. But sometimes being a founder means figuring out how to effectively carve out time to address critical issues. No matter how busy you are. So that’s why I’m shoving that guilt way down and asking you to take this one: All Raise — led by Portland’s own Paige Hendrix Buckner — is running their annual community survey. ... More When every thing on the Web is AI training fodder It’s always super interesting to discuss AI. So many folks are enamoured of the potential. So many folks are figuring out ways to incorporate it into day-to-day activities. And so many folks are super concerned about the every growing number of virtual vacuums that run roughshod across the Web, tirelessly scraping the Internet for data to train their models. ... More 💐 Silicon Florist link arrangements Mar 23: AI productivity gains, cofounder vetting, AWS cloud empire, and an Oregon company scandal Mar 24: AI tools, humanoid robots, startup funding trends, and Portland tech events Mar 25: fjScaler raises $7M, Women.Money. Power. Mar 26: Thoughts on slowing the fuck down Mar 27: Oregon economic challenges, AI developments, and community events Photo by Linneas Boland-Godbey
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/silicon-florist-more-qsbs-bs-rick-turoczy-ycw0e?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BYYpwcpvDR9y1zep%2FQMvMCw%3D%3D&midToken=AQGEpmBPZVOktg&midSig=2HGYGqpBsnsYc1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-4xcaz4~mni39zk7~mt-null-null&eid=4xcaz4-mni39zk7-mt
  
      

----------------------------------------

This email was intended for Benjamin Barber (Civil Rights Litigator and Software Engineer
)

[Customer Support That Scales With Us. Click here to invest now.](https://invest.misorobotics.com/)

Hi Benjamin Barber,
 
Miso announced today a new nationwide support partnership with AwesomeOS, a proven leader in multilingual technical robotics support and customer experience operations. This collaboration expands our ability to deliver top-quality 24/7 virtual customer support for our Flippy Fry Station AI robots as we work to scale their deployments across the country.

Rebecca Davis, Miso’s Vice President of Operations, said, “As Flippy’s adoption continues to grow, world-class support becomes just as critical as world-class technology. AwesomeOS allows us to scale customer support with speed, consistency, and nationwide reach.” Davis added, “Just as partners like NVIDIA, Uber AI, and Amazon have helped to strengthened our AI and cloud infrastructure, AwesomeOS strengthens the human layer of our platform—ensuring customers get fast, reliable support in the languages and time zones they operate in.”

Lexi Schneider, AwesomeOS’ Head of North American Marketing, said, “Miso’s vision for intelligent, connected restaurant kitchens aligns directly with how we think about global customer experience at scale. This partnership reflects a shared commitment to reliability, operational excellence, and long-term growth as Flippy deployments expand.”

Why it matters:

24/7, Multilingual Coverage
Flippy’s restaurant operators get real-time support across regions, languages, and service hours.

Faster, More Cost-Efficient Scale
Partnering with a proven support team allows us to expand our support capacity without building expensive internal teams—helping control costs, while boosting quality.

Stronger Customer Experience
Faster resolution times improve uptime, operator confidence, and long-term retention for our products.

[For Investors Like You:](https://invest.misorobotics.com/)
Strategic partnerships with industry leaders are one of the fastest, most capital-efficient ways we can scale—expanding capability, while driving long-term enterprise value.

And you can be part of our growth. New and returning investors can still access up to 7% bonus shares.

[INVEST NOW](https://invest.misorobotics.com/checkout)

Together with AwesomeOS, we’re building the future of restaurant kitchen intelligence.

To growth,
—The Miso Team

https://invest.misorobotics.com/

© 2026 - Miso Robotics® all rights reserved.

Miso Robotics is offering securities through the use of an Offering Statement that has been qualified by the Securities and Exchange Commission under Tier II of Regulation A. A copy of the Final Offering Circular that forms a part of the Offering Statement may be obtained from: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1710670/000110465926001520/tm262179d1_partiiandiii.htm

No longer want to receive these emails? [Unsubscribe](https://manage.kmail-lists.com/subscriptions/unsubscribe?a=LqAUi4&c=01GJ2JY24V0AXTV2CK5DYYX9P6&k=7f7540ba9c98acd58d909fdae016566b&m=01KN0640P1DGQ2AXSWH2XN9FN9&r=01KN7CD38ZDEM447FA4RZFPX4F).
Miso Robotics 650 E. Green Street Pasadena, CA 91101

So when you say "accepted" do you mean you want the priority mail tracking number, or do you want a LITERAL IRS officer to certify it? The estimated wait time to get to see an IRS employee at the Taxpayer Assistance Center to get such a certification is 30-60 days, which would certainly put us past the time that we're supposed to vacate the residence. This seems to be opposite of the guidance that I was presented by your office less than a year ago with Ariana Stalcup, that contains the actual income verification requirements. I have no idea what the process is like to get it "verified, complete,
accepted by the IRS".

So when you say "accepted" do you mean you want the priority mail tracking
number, or do you want a LITERAL IRS officer to certify it? The estimated
wait time to get to see an IRS employee at the Taxpayer Assistance Center
to get such a certification is 30-60 days, which would certainly put us
past the time that we're supposed to vacate the residence. This seems to be
opposite of the guidance that I was presented by your office less than a
year ago with Ariana Stalcup, that contains the actual income verification
requirements. It's also amusing quite frankly, because not once have I ever
seen your office demand Julio Cortez ever produce an IRS 1040, much less
have some sort of acceptance letter from the IRS, given that he claims he

Be part of what’s next

  * DLCP Joins District Leaders at the 12th Annual DMPED March Madness [ #link_3 ] 
  * Connecting With Students at the Washington School for Girls [ #link_4 ] 
  * DLCP in the Community | Outreach Highlights [ #link_9 ] 
  * Apply Now for Sidewalk Vending Near Nationals Park This Baseball Season [ #link_8 ] 
  * What You Need to Know | 2026 License Renewals [ #link_13 ] 
  * Legislative Updates | In Case You Missed It [ #link_5 ] 
  * Contact Us [ #link_2 ] 

A Message from the Director

tiffany  [ https://dlcp.dc.gov/jobs ] 

Dear Customers, Colleagues, and Friends,

Spring has officially arrived, bringing with it a renewed sense of momentum across the District. This season always reminds me that growth doesn’t happen all at once—it happens through steady progress, new partnerships, and the collective energy of our community. That spirit of forward movement has been at the center of our work this month.

I recently attended the 12th Annual DMPED March Madness, an event that brings together District agencies, developers, and community partners to preview upcoming projects and investments across DC. It was important for DLCP to be in the room, not only to stay aligned with the District’s economic development priorities but also to help position the businesses we serve to benefit from the growth ahead. You can read more about our participation below.

This year’s Women Mean Business Summit, I joined an inspiring group of leaders and entrepreneurs. During my remarks, I shared where we are—and where we’re going—in both the federal and local business landscape. It was a moment to highlight the progress we’ve made, the challenges we continue to navigate, and the opportunities emerging for women‑owned businesses across the District. You’ll find more about that below as well.

This month also included the introduction of the Short‑Term Rental Regulation Amendment Act of 2026 [ https://mayor.dc.gov/sites/default/files/dc/sites/mayormb/publication/attachments/ShortTermRentalAmendementActof2026.pdf ], which modernizes the District’s short‑term rental rules and expands opportunities for residents. The bill creates a new special event license, clarifies definitions, and streamlines existing requirements. You can read more in our press release here [ https://dlcp.dc.gov/release/mayor-bowser-announces-new-short-term-rental-legislation-create-more-economic-opportunities ].

As we close out Women’s History Month, I encourage you to visit the DLCP Hub [ https://dlcp.dc.gov/blog/femme-fatale-dc-tribute-women-who-create-and-lead ] to read our feature on Femme Fatale DC co‑owners Cee Smith and Adriana Mendoza, who reflect the creativity and resilience we celebrate this month.

I am proud that DLCP is helping expand access, opportunity, and support for women in business, and I look forward to the continued progress we will make together. 

 
signature

Tiffany Crowe
Director

________________________________________________________________________

 

Consumer News You Can Use

Spring brings an increase in marketplace activity across the District, and DLCP’s Consumer Protection Unit is sharing important reminders to help residents make informed decisions and avoid deceptive practices. Below are two key advisories now available on our website.

Transparency at Used Car Dealerships 

The District is home to more than 40 licensed used car dealerships, and transparency is essential when purchasing a vehicle. DLCP is reminding consumers and dealerships about required price disclosures, federal “Buyer’s Guide” r
documents.title: Email import: DLCP News – March | Momentum, Leadership, and Community Impact

documents.content:
Subject: Top Stories
Folder: [Gmail]/All Mail
From: Prohuman Foundation | Top Stories <beprohuman+top-stories@substack.com>
To: starworks5@gmail.com
Date: Sun, 29 Mar 2026 12:02:38 +0000
Matched addresses: all mailbox messages
Artifact directory: /home/barberb/HACC/evidence/email_imports/solomon-live-fetch-2026/starworks5/gmail-import/0000000-0000999/msg_20260329120238.3.a17e87607d54919e-mg-d1.substack.com_20260329_Top-Stories

View this post on the web at https://beprohuman.substack.com/p/top-stories-359

We know too much about each other
Prohuman Foundation [ https://substack.com/redirect/2c927ccd-2c99-4517-bf30-71a06a765176?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] advisor Katherine Brodsky [ https://substack.com/redirect/de636e72-343f-4290-8513-7a92491bd8f4?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] writes in Random Minds that the internet and social media have made us more connected, yet more divided by hiding complexity behind fragments of opinions, bios, and avatars. Citing new research on the polarizing effects of too much shallow connectivity, she argues that the best solution is to focus on more in-person presence and real shared experiences.
Thanks to the Internet and social media, we are more connected than ever. But we are also more divided. I think it’s making us hate each other not because of what it exposes us to, but because of what it hides. We see too much of what people think, and not nearly enough of who they are.
Rediscovering Knowledge as the Key to Reading
Two champions of knowledge-rich instruction reflect on its current momentum
For Education Next, Daniel T. Willingham and E.D. Hirsch, Jr. explain why American educators have returned to knowledge-rich reading instruction. Arguing that shared background knowledge is essential for reading comprehension, the authors predict that sequenced, knowledge-building curricula will improve reading outcomes (especially for disadvantaged students) while building civic understanding.
For decades, reading instruction in the United States has focused on helping children acquire generic, transferable skills such as finding the main idea of a passage or drawing inferences. At the same time, educators have minimized the contribution that knowledge makes to reading—the idea that knowing something about the topic of a passage helps a reader make sense of it. Advocates for the skills-based approach have argued that children clearly needed to learn skills, but they didn’t have to store much knowledge in memory; they could always look it up, especially in the digital age. Contending that children need to learn some facts made one seem a nostalgic fuddy-duddy.
But something has changed, and knowledge is having a moment in education fashion.
Young Men Aren’t Checked Out. We’ve Closed the Paths That Once Guided Them
Young men are not “checked out” or rejecting adulthood, writes Samuel J. Abrams. Instead, clear pathways, like stable jobs without degrees, vocational routes, and formative community and religious organizations, have narrowed or vanished, leaving a growing gap between aspirations and attainment. The solution, he argues in AEIdeas, lies in restoring dignified alternatives like apprenticeships and mentorship structures, not redefining manhood.
For the better part of a decade, a familiar narrative has taken hold: Young men are adrift—detached from work, disconnected from relationships, and increasingly uninterested in the responsibilities of adulthood.
It is a compelling story. It is also incomplete.
Data from the Institute for Family Studies (IFS), based on a survey of men aged 18 to 29, tells a far more complicated and revealing story: Young men are not rejecting responsibility. They are not abandoning the idea of adulthood. If anything, they are holding onto it. . . .
Large majorities still aspire to marriage and fatherhood. They define manhood in traditional terms: responsibility, sacrifice, and the capacity to provide for others.
These are not the answers of a generation in retreat. They are the answers of a generation that still believes in obligation. Yet their lives tell a different story.
Can local news save us?
We may have lost our faith in legacy news, but there’s one news source that’s continued to have our backs and our trust.
Writing in her own Spirit & Sword Substack, Alexandra Vollman describes how local news r

>
>
>
> On Thu, Mar 26, 2026 at 1:58 PM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Sure, you can go ahead and try this informal hearing, but it seems to me
>> like just another excuse to delay, and it just sounds like the matter is
>> going to have to go before a judge.
>>
>>
>> With regards to the reasonable accommodation about the April 1st deadline
>> to vacate:
>>
>> In November there was a contested restraining order hearing, and the
>> restraining order was continued against julio
>>
>> In December I had placed an application to the blossom, which they sat on
>> and did not send to you, which amounts to "lemon dropping" (the reverse of
>> cherry picking), and what I suspect to be "lemon dropping" based on
>> protected immutable characteristics given that is what employees claim
>> happen on glassdoor, and what your office promotes as an official policy,
>> in violation of 42 usc 1981
>> https://dochub.clackamas.us/documents/drupal/ed77538a-1d00-45f2-b95b-9aecca79e5f3
>>
>> In January early I had received the 90 days notice and told your office
>> that I wanted the tenant protection voucher, after incorrectly stating I
>> had not put an application into blossom, which your office had also
>> confirmed.
>>
>> In January your office violated the court order by removing me from the
>> lease and putting Julio Cortez back on the lease
>>
>> In March you tried to meet your obligations to retain an apartment for
>> us, by giving us a 3rd floor apartment with stairs by the same company that
>> runs the blossom.
>>
>> Only last week did you finally provide me with a tenant protection
>> voucher, so that I could actually move out, 12 days from the day from which
>> I was supposed to be evicted, so in effect the conditions you want to
>> complain about are circumstances of your own creation.
>>
>>
>> Thus, my reasonable accommodation would be based on the illusory promise,
>> promissory estoppel, frustration of purpose, force majure, and unclean
>> hands.
>>
>> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/force_majeure
>> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/frustration_of_purpose
>> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/illusory_promise
>> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/promissory_estoppel
>> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/clean-hands_doctrine
>>
>>
>> [image: image.png]
>>
>> On Thu, Mar 26, 2026 at 12:23 PM Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us>
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hello,
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> If you disagree with the decision, you may request an informal hearing
>>> as outlined in the denial letter.
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> Please clarify what your reasonable accommodation request is in the
>>> following statement: “I also formally request a reasonable accommodation,
>>> given that the tenant protection voucher was only received last week, due
>>> to delays and violations of the violence against women act, by trying to
>>> remove me from the lease and keeping Julio on the lease, in violation of a
>>> court order, as the proximal cause of delays.”
>>>
>>> Thank you,
>>>
>>>
>>> Kati
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> Kati Tilton
>>>
>>> 971-201-8582
>>>
>>> Operations Manager, Housing Authority of Clackamas County
>>>
>>>
>

wrote:

> All of the work is performed on behalf of charity.
>
> On Fri, Mar 27, 2026 at 2:50 PM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>>
>>
>> On Fri, Mar 27, 2026 at 2:46 PM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>>>
>>>
>>> On Thu, Mar 26, 2026 at 2:37 PM RA_Portability <
>>> Portability@homeforward.org> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hello Jane,
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Your portability request was received to transfer your voucher to
>>>> Multnomah County.  The next step is to send you the Home Forward intake
>>>> packet.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Please respond to this email or call at the number below, to let us
>>>> know how we can send you the intake packet; mail, email, or you can pick it
>>>> up person.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Please let us know as soon as possible.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Thank you
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> *Stephanie Taylor*
>>>>
>>>> Program Supervisor
>>>>
>>>> Rent Assistance
>>>>
>>>> Pronouns: she/her (*Pronouns.org Resources on Personal Pronouns
>>>> <https://pronouns.org/>*)
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> 135 SW Ash St.
>>>>
>>>> Portland, Oregon 97204
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> *tel:* 503.802.8443
>>>>
>>>> *fax:* 503.802.8330
>>>>
>>>> *tty*: 503.802.8554
>>>>
>>>> *web:* www.homeforward.org
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Extensions must be submitted in writing before the voucher expires.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Reporting household changes must be in writing. Forms are available on
>>>> Home Forward’s website. On the homepage, enter keywords: Housing
>>>> program>housing choice voucher>participant>household changes. You can
>>>> also request to have the forms mailed to you.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> You can pick up forms or drop off packets at our customer service
>>>> window.
>>>>
>>>> The customer service window is open Tuesday – Thursday from 9:00AM –
>>>> noon and 1:00PM - 3:00PM at 135 SW Ash Street
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Confidentiality Notice: This communication may contain privileged or
>>>> other confidential information exempt from disclosure. If you are not the

On Thu, Mar 26, 2026 at 1:58 PM benjamin barber <starworks5@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Sure, you can go ahead and try this informal hearing, but it seems to me
> like just another excuse to delay, and it just sounds like the matter is
> going to have to go before a judge.
>
>
> With regards to the reasonable accommodation about the April 1st deadline
> to vacate:
>
> In November there was a contested restraining order hearing, and the
> restraining order was continued against julio
>
> In December I had placed an application to the blossom, which they sat on
> and did not send to you, which amounts to "lemon dropping" (the reverse of
> cherry picking), and what I suspect to be "lemon dropping" based on
> protected immutable characteristics given that is what employees claim
> happen on glassdoor, and what your office promotes as an official policy,
> in violation of 42 usc 1981
> https://dochub.clackamas.us/documents/drupal/ed77538a-1d00-45f2-b95b-9aecca79e5f3
>
> In January early I had received the 90 days notice and told your office
> that I wanted the tenant protection voucher, after incorrectly stating I
> had not put an application into blossom, which your office had also
> confirmed.
>
> In January your office violated the court order by removing me from the
> lease and putting Julio Cortez back on the lease
>
> In March you tried to meet your obligations to retain an apartment for us,
> by giving us a 3rd floor apartment with stairs by the same company that
> runs the blossom.
>
> Only last week did you finally provide me with a tenant protection
> voucher, so that I could actually move out, 12 days from the day from which
> I was supposed to be evicted, so in effect the conditions you want to
> complain about are circumstances of your own creation.
>
>
> Thus, my reasonable accommodation would be based on the illusory promise,
> promissory estoppel, frustration of purpose, force majure, and unclean
> hands.
>
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/force_majeure
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/frustration_of_purpose
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/illusory_promise
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/promissory_estoppel
> https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/clean-hands_doctrine
>
>
> [image: image.png]
>
> On Thu, Mar 26, 2026 at 12:23 PM Tilton, Kati <KTilton@clackamas.us>
> wrote:
>
>> Hello,
>>
>>
>>
>> If you disagree with the decision, you may request an informal hearing as
>> outlined in the denial letter.
>>
>>
>>
>> Please clarify what your reasonable accommodation request is in the
>> following statement: “I also formally request a reasonable accommodation,
>> given that the tenant protection voucher was only received last week, due
>> to delays and violations of the violence against women act, by trying to
>> remove me from the lease and keeping Julio on the lease, in violation of a
>> court order, as the proximal cause of delays.”
>>
>> Thank you,
>>
>>
>> Kati
>>
>>
>>
>> Kati Tilton
>>
>> 971-201-8582
>>
>> Operations Manager, Housing Authority of Clackamas County
>>
>>

------------------------------------------------------------

By Taxpayers Association of Oregon OregonWatchdog.com The Oregonian has a story about the dramatic change in Multnomah County taxpayer migration since a big income tax increase was passed in 2020. The Preschool-for-All ballot measure raised income taxes by 1.5% to 3%.  A second big income tax was passed in the same year with the Homeless […]

Last fall, I wrote about an instance where a lawyer was caught using fake citations that were generated from artificial intelligence. It happened again in Oregon District Court, where Magistrate Judge Mark Clarke sanctioned a lawyer fairly severely in Couvrette v. Wisnovsky.
